description: A clean, minimalist designed graphic card on a plain white background formatted to mimic a news-article headline / social share card. At the top center is a small red rectangular tag/label with white uppercase text reading 'COAST GUARD' (a section/kicker label). Below it, in large bold black serif-ish sans display type, is the headline: 'DOGE helps Coast Guard save nearly $33M by eliminating ineffective IT program' (with 'ineffective IT program' set off in single quotes). Beneath the headline, in smaller gray regular weight text, is a subhead/standfirst: 'Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem praised the move in a statement to Fox News Digital, calling it Another win for government efficiency at DHS!' The design is text-only with no photos, people, or agency seals. This is a propaganda/messaging graphic styled as a media excerpt to lend third-party credibility, promoting the DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) cost-cutting narrative and Secretary Noem. Messaging technique: borrowed authority of a news outlet and emphasis on dollar savings. Target audience: taxpayers and supporters of government efficiency / the Trump administration's DOGE agenda.
notable_text: COAST GUARD; DOGE helps Coast Guard save nearly $33M by eliminating 'ineffective IT program'; 'Another win for government efficiency at DHS!'
